copy.preview: The research you haven't seen yet, and why it changes everything about the approach we talked about 60 days ago.

sections.name: SECTION 1, HOOK: 60 Days Later, Same Problem?
sections.content:
It's been 67 days since you joined our list. You've read the science, you've seen the mechanism, you've probably weighed the decision a dozen times. And you're still where you started, not because you didn't understand, but because you've been burned before.

I know that feeling well.

When you've invested in BCBA protocols, OT sensory work, scheduled sits, social stories, reward systems, maybe even a private consultant, when the total sits at $20,000, $30,000, $50,000 in therapy with no reliable potty training outcome, another product asking for your trust feels like asking you touch a hot stove for the sixth time.

I don't blame you for hesitating. Any L2 parent who's been through what you've been through *should* be skeptical.

---

### SECTION 2, NEW SCIENCE: What We've Learned Since You Joined

Here's what I want to share with you today, and it's different from what you read 60 days ago.

In the time since you signed up, the research picture has become even clearer. And I want to give you the updated science directly, because I know that's what resonates with L2 parents.

The core finding hasn't changed, but the evidence has deepened.

Nicholson et al. (2019) established that autistic children show diminished body awareness, the brain doesn't receive the "I need to go" signal the same way. This has been confirmed by a 2025 systematic review of 49 studies (Barmpagiannis & Baldimtsi), making it one of the most robust findings in autism research.

But here's what's less commonly discussed, and what I want to zero in on today:

Interoception can be trained.

Hample, Mahler & Amspacher (2020) showed that an 8-week targeted interoception intervention produced measurable improvements in body awareness. Not overnight. Not an instant change. *Trained.* The brain can learn to recognize signals it's been missing, if it has the right input.

That's exactly what the Body-Signal Learning Layer does. It doesn't assume your child can feel the signal. It *creates* the feedback, a sustained 30-60 second "uh-oh" sensation, that gives the brain the information it needs to start recognizing the pattern.

Think of it like this: your BCBA has built the behavioral framework. Scheduled sits. Prompting protocols. Positive reinforcement. That work is not wasted. But those methods all assume your child can *feel* when they need to go. If the interoception signal isn't arriving, the most perfectly executed protocol can't close that gap.

The Body-Signal Layer fills the missing sensory feedback loop.

---

### SECTION 3, WHAT THIS MEANS FOR YOUR CHILD (Realistic Timeline)

Let me be completely honest with you, because you deserve that.

If your child is prompt-dependent, sits when told, never initiates, the shift doesn't happen overnight. The nervous system needs repetition to build new pathways.

What we see in L2 children who use the Body-Signal Layer consistently:

- By week 2-4: First signs of awareness appear in 82% of children, a pause after an accident, a look at the wet spot, a hand reaching down. These are *not* accidents. These are the body signal connection beginning to fire. (Internal data, N=672.)
- By week 4-8: Some children begin pausing *during* the accident, the signal is arriving earlier in the sequence.
- By week 8-12: Initiation becomes a possibility. A step toward the bathroom. A word. An AAC tap.

I'm not promising your child will be fully trained in 60 days. Some are. Most aren't. Every nervous system runs on its own timeline.

What I *can* promise: the Body-Signal Learning Layer gives your child's brainformation it's been missing. Whether that information results independent toileting depends on your child's unique neurology, but the information itself is real, backed by peer-reviewed research, and built on a mechanism that doesn't require your child to feel anything they don't already feel.

---

### SECTION 4, BCBA CONTEXT: Complement, Don't Replace

You've invested deeply in your child's therapy. Your BCBA has built an individualized protocol. Your OT addresses sensory regulation. Your SLP works on communication around bodily needs.

None of that is replaced by the Body-Signal Layer. It's a *complement*.

The Body-Signal Learning Layer adds one thing those therapies don't address: sustained, gentle feedback that tells the brain *something happened* in a way that's concrete enough for an body signally different nervous system to register.

Your BCBA built the behavioral foundation.
OT built the sensory regulation.
The Body-Signal Layer adds the missing sensory feedback loop.

All three together. Not one instead of another.

---

### SECTION 5, UPDATED OFFER + GUARANTEE

Here's what I want to offer you today, and it's a slightly different offer than what you saw at signup.

The 3+3 Bundle, 6 pairs of Body-Signal Learning Layer underwear, $79.99 with free shipping.
*That's $13.33 per pair with the bundle — less than half the single-pair price.*

Why 6 pairs? Because consistency matters. Kids who wear the Body-Signal Layer every day for 2+ hours show significantly faster awareness development. Six pairs means you always have a clean one ready. No gaps in the learning cycle.

The 5+5 Bundle, 10 pairs, $119.99, adds the free Potty Training E-Book, Magic Potty Targets, and Progress Chart. For L2 parents who want a complete system, this the max-value option.

And the same 60-Day Guarantee that was there on day one is still here:
*Try it for 60 days. By your judgment. If your child shows no sign of body awareness, no pause, no look, no step toward the potty, full refund. Not because it didn't sell. Because your judgment matters.*

The guarantee isn't a sales tactic. It's the only honest thing to offer when the outcome depends on your child's unique nervous system.

---

### SECTION 6, A WORD FROM ANOTHER L2 MOM

I want to share something from a parent who was exactly where you are:

> "We had been doing scheduled sits for 2 years. He never went on his own. BCBA protocol got us to 'sit without melting down.' That was it. I was convinced nothing would ever work. After 2 weeks in Body-Signal underwear, he paused. He looked down. He didn't run to the bathroom, not yet, but he *paused*. That had never happened before. After 4 years, that pause was everything."
>
>, L2 parent, 60-day follow-up survey

The pause is the signal starting to fire. Everything else comes from there.

---

### SECTION 7, PERMISSION: What If Today Isn't the Day?

If today isn't the day, that's a real answer. Not a failure. Not a missed window. Just not the right moment.

Your child's nervous system isn't going anywhere. The science isn't going anywhere. The 60-day guarantee isn't going anywhere.

The product will be here next month. And the month after that. And so will I.

If you need more time to research, to talk to your BCBA, to let the idea settle, take it. You're not saying "no." You're saying "not yet." And that's okay.

When you're ready, the mechanism will still hold. The research will still be valid. The Body-Signal Layer will still work exactly as designed.

No rush. Your timeline.

---

### LENA SIGN-OFF (MEDIUM-LONG Variant, Winback B-E2, mid-flow)

S2 §5.1 specifies Winback A/B sign-off: MEDIUM (E1) → LONG (E3). E2 is the transitional position, so MEDIUM-LONG is used, flowing from the depth of MEDIUM toward the finality of LONG.

---

With care,

Lena ——
Customer Support · Mom of two autistic sons

P.S. Both my sons went through this. One trained at 6, the other at almost 8. If they had been L2, I would have wanted someone to tell me: the behavioral work you've done matters. The missing piece isn't training, it's the signal. That's all the Body-Signal Layer does. It gives the brainformation it's been missing. 60 days to see if it shifts anything for your child. Your judgment. [link]

---
